packs.situation: When a Dutch person switches to English the moment you try to speak Dutch
packs.persona: Belonging-motivated expat stuck in the Shame Cycle (Monika, Molly, Nina)
packs.priority_reason: Deepest, most-repeated emotional pain across the interviews; highly relatable and shareable, strong send potential

posts.hook: When they switch to English, say this
posts.angle: Learner tries Dutch, the other person flips to English; learner uses one calm line to keep it in Dutch while Joost slows down and stays in Dutch
posts.source: barrier: Shame Cycle; monika 'people instantly switch to English'

posts.hook: 5 polite ways to keep them speaking Dutch to you
posts.angle: Exact phrases to ask someone to stay in Dutch and slow down, without it being awkward

posts.hook: They just switched to English on you. What now?
posts.angle: Three realistic responses; ask 'has this happened to you and what did you actually say?' to pull sentence-length comments

posts.hook: Why people keep switching to English on you (and how to break the loop)
posts.angle: Pull the shame-cycle angle from the article into a short reel; transform, do not paste

packs.situation: The A2 inburgering speaking exam (spreken): why people keep getting a 5
packs.persona: Inburgering candidate with exam anxiety + Production Gap (Facebook groups, Nina)
packs.priority_reason: The dominant blocker for the core inburgering buyer; aligns with an existing article/brief; high search intent

posts.hook: Why you keep failing the A2 speaking exam
posts.angle: Candidate over-complicates an answer and stumbles, then uses short correct sentences and gets through; Joost guides. Frame the exam as 'challenging for many', never 'the hardest'
posts.source: facebook 'mostly grammar, short sentences to avoid mistakes'

posts.hook: Short Dutch sentences that get you through spreken
posts.angle: Safe, simple sentence frames that avoid grammar traps under time pressure

posts.hook: Two answers to the same exam question. Which is safer?
posts.angle: A long risky answer vs a short clean one; ask 'which would you say, and why?'

posts.hook: The real reason the speaking exam trips people up
posts.angle: Pull one angle from the speaking-exam brief into a reel or carousel

packs.situation: Getting through the supermarket checkout in Dutch without switching to English
packs.priority_reason: Universal daily situation, easy to film, broad top-of-funnel appeal and save value

posts.hook: Get through the Dutch checkout without freezing
posts.angle: Joost as the cashier runs the usual checkout questions (bonuskaart, pinnen, bonnetje, tasje); shopper answers each one
posts.source: molly 'so I don't have to switch to English in grocery stores'

posts.hook: 8 things the cashier asks (and what to say back)
posts.angle: The exact checkout phrases and your one-line answers

posts.hook: The cashier asks 'Wilt u de bon?' What now?
posts.angle: Three answers; ask 'what do you always say at the checkout?'

posts.hook: Rehearse the checkout out loud before you go
posts.angle: Off-theme flex: pull the 'practice speaking alone' angle and tie it to rehearsing one real situation

packs.situation: Calling or visiting the huisarts (doctor) in Dutch without freezing
packs.priority_reason: Concrete, named situation with real stakes; strong save value, slightly narrower than supermarket

posts.hook: How to call the Dutch doctor without freezing
posts.angle: Phone call to the huisarts; Joost as receptionist asks the standard questions; learner books an appointment

posts.hook: Booking a doctor's appointment in Dutch: the exact lines
posts.angle: Phrases for making the appointment and describing what is wrong

posts.hook: The receptionist asks 'Waar heeft u last van?'
posts.angle: What is she asking and how would you answer; ask people to reply with their own answer

posts.hook: Why you understand Dutch but freeze when you speak
posts.angle: Off-theme flex: production-gap angle, transformed into a short carousel

packs.situation: Total beginner overwhelmed by the inburgering exam: where do I even start?
packs.persona: Work-visa procrastinator / overwhelmed beginner with Schedule Constraints (Facebook groups, Pattern B)
packs.priority_reason: Strong top-of-funnel beginner magnet; aligns with two existing briefs; slightly less scene-driven for the reel

posts.hook: Starting Dutch from zero? Do this first
posts.angle: Overwhelmed beginner asks Joost 'Waar moet ik beginnen?'; Joost reassures and names one simple first step
posts.source: facebook 'beginners don't know where to start and feel alone'

posts.hook: The 5 inburgering exam parts, explained simply
posts.angle: A beginner's map of reading, listening, writing, speaking, KNM (ties to all-parts-in-one differentiator)

posts.hook: How many Dutch words do you actually need to pass A2?
posts.angle: Multiple choice; ask people to guess and say why before the reveal

posts.hook: A simple first-week plan for inburgering from scratch
posts.angle: Pull the roadmap from the where-to-start brief into a carousel

packs.situation: The school gate (schoolplein): small talk with other parents and your child's teacher at drop-off and pickup
packs.persona: Family/Belonging-motivated expat mom, partner visa, school-age kids (Alysa, Pattern A) -- Shame Cycle + Access-to-practice barriers
packs.priority_reason: Scene-rich daily mom situation, high save value, and it fills the under-served family-motivated-mom persona the current backlog misses; grounded in a repeated pain (kids' friends and their parents don't speak English)

posts.hook: The 30 seconds at the school gate that freeze every expat mom
posts.angle: Joost plays a relaxed Dutch parent at pickup; an expat mom does the small talk -- names, ages, which groep, a playdate -- in short correct A1 lines instead of switching to English
posts.source: alysa 'with the friends of my children they don't speak much English'; Pattern A family scrutiny

posts.hook: What to say at the school gate (parent edition)
posts.angle: The exact lines to greet another parent, ask about their kid, and arrange a playdate, plus a few for the juf/meester

posts.hook: The juf says 'Hij heeft lekker gespeeld vandaag.' What now?
posts.angle: Three reads of a teacher's pickup line; ask 'what is the juf actually telling you, and how would you reply?' to pull sentence-length comments

posts.hook: You understand the other parents but freeze when it's your turn
posts.angle: Off-theme flex: pull the production-gap angle into a short carousel framed for parents; transform, do not paste
posts.source: outputs/articles/passed-inburgering-cant-speak-dutch; anna 'I can only say: I have a green apple'

packs.situation: The consultatiebureau (baby/child health clinic) visit in Dutch -- and starting to use simple Dutch with your own little one
packs.persona: New mom, self-employed, sleep-deprived with a baby/toddler (Anna, the Facebook mom thread) -- Schedule Constraints + Family motivation
packs.priority_reason: Emotionally unique to the brand (talk to your child as they grow up Dutch-speaking) and a named high-stakes situation every NL parent of a young kid faces; slightly narrower audience than the school gate, hence medium

posts.hook: Your consultatiebureau visit, but this time in Dutch
posts.angle: Joost as the consultatiebureau nurse runs the standard baby-check questions (sleeping, eating, growth); the mom answers each one in short A1 lines instead of freezing
posts.source: anna new mother JTBD; facebook mom thread; named NL situation

posts.hook: Consultatiebureau Dutch: what the nurse asks about your baby
posts.angle: The exact questions about sleeping, feeding, and growth with your one-line answers, plus a few easy lines to start using Dutch with your own baby at home
posts.source: anna 'talk to my son as he grows up Dutch-speaking'; facebook 'Dutch cartoons/songs with their kids'

posts.hook: The nurse asks 'Slaapt ze goed door?' -- what is she really asking?
posts.angle: Three options; ask 'how is your little one's sleep -- answer in Dutch if you dare' for personal-angle comments, never bare A/B

posts.hook: Learn Dutch in the cracks of a parenting day (no extra hour needed)
posts.angle: Off-theme flex: micro-habits for moms with no time -- 5am, lunch break, Dutch songs with the kids -- transformed from the practice-alone article and the Facebook thread
posts.source: facebook 'micro steps daily', 'no one ever has time'; outputs/articles/how-to-practice-dutch-speaking-alone
